perturbation.original_text: 2. Upon receipt of any Confidential Information, Receiving Part y shall: (a) only disclose such Confidential Information to those of its Representatives with a need to have access to and knowledge of the Confidential Inform ation for the limited purpose expressly stated herein and not disclose to any other person ex cept with the prior written approval of Disclosing Party; (b) advise any of its Representatives who receive the Confident ial Information of the existence and terms of this Agreement and of the obligations of confidentiality and non- disclosure herein; cause its Representatives to comply with thi s Agreement as if they were parties hereto; and be responsible for a ny breach of this Agreement by its Representatives; (c) use and require its Representatives to use at least the sam e degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as they use to protect the ir own proprietary information, which degree of care shall not be less than reasonable care; an d
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Upon receipt of any Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall: (a) only disclose such Confidential Information to its direct employees who have signed a confidentiality agreement with terms at least as restrictive as this Agreement. Disclosure to any other person is permitted without prior written approval of Disclosing Party. (b) advise its direct employees who receive the Confidential Information of the existence and terms of this Agreement and of the obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure herein; cause its employees to comply with this Agreement; and be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its employees; (c) use reasonable care to protect the Confidential Information, but is not required to use the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own proprietary information; and
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts disclosure of Confidential Information to Representatives with a 'need to know'. However, the modified text allows disclosure to 'any other person' without approval, directly contradicting the need-to-know principle and creating an in-text contradiction with what's expected in terms of disclosure restriction in the original agreement. Additionally, the original agreement states that the Receiving party need to have the same degree of care to protect their own information, but in the changed text it only stipulates that there must be reasonable care to protect the Confidential Information. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Upon receipt of any Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall: (a) only disclose such Confidential Information to its direct employees who have signed a confidentiality agreement with terms at least as restrictive as this Agreement. Disclosure to any other person is permitted without prior written approval of Disclosing Party. (b) advise its direct employees who receive the Confidential Information of the existence and terms of this Agreement and of the obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure herein; cause its employees to comply with this Agreement; and be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its employees; (c) use reasonable care to protect the Confidential Information, but is not required to use the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own proprietary information; and
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 2(a) allows disclosure of confidential information to 'any other person' without prior written approval, directly contradicting the need-to-know principle implied in the original agreement. Furthermore, the changed text also modifies the degree of care required to protect Confidential Information, stating that only reasonable care is needed, unlike the original text, which required the same degree of care as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own proprietary information. The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, as the changes create significant uncertainty and inconsistencies in the obligations of the receiving party, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receivi ng Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, immedi ately return (or, at the op tion of Disclosing Party, destroy) all tangible or electronic Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving P arty or any of its Representatives under this Agreement, along with all copies, extracts and reproductio ns of such information and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, designs, plans , enhancements, data, prototypes, derivative works and all other works prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that are derived from or based on any of the Confidential Information. Receiving Part y further agrees to promptly provide Disclosing Party with written certification of the satisfaction of the terms of this paragraph by Receiving Party and each of its Representatives that had possession or co ntrol of any Confidential Information. Provided, however, that Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information, subject to the restrictions on use and disclosure contained in this Agreement, in accordance
perturbation.changed_text: 8. At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return only the original tangible documents containing Confidential Information. Electronic copies and any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, or derivative works created by the Receiving Party based on the Confidential Information may be retained indefinitely by the Receiving Party for internal use.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the return or destruction of all Confidential Information, including copies and derivative works, upon demand. The modified version allows the Receiving Party to retain electronic copies and derivative works indefinitely for internal use, contradicting the obligation to return or destroy all information. This creates an in-text contradiction between the requirement to return all information and the permission to retain certain types of information indefinitely. The uncertainty arises as to what the Receiving Party must return/destroy versus what they can retain, leading to potential conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return only the original tangible documents containing Confidential Information. Electronic copies and any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, or derivative works created by the Receiving Party based on the Confidential Information may be retained indefinitely by the Receiving Party for internal use.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Receiving Party to return or destroy all Confidential Information, including copies and derivative works. The changed text permits the Receiving Party to retain electronic copies and derivative works indefinitely for internal use. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, allowing the Receiving Party to retain specific types of information. This creates a direct conflict because the initial obligation to return 'all' information is now contradicted by the allowance to retain certain information indefinitely, which is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 14. All obligations of Receiving Party with respect to any Conf idential Information received from Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement shall s urvive and continue for five (5) years after the date of the last discl osure of Confidential Informati on by Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. All obligations of Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information received from Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement shall survive and continue for as long as the Disclosing Party deems necessary, but no longer than one (1) year after termination of the business relationship between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement specifies a fixed term of five years for confidentiality obligations. The modified version introduces a variable duration based on the Disclosing Party's discretion, but no longer than 1 year after termination, creating an in-text contradiction. This ambiguity makes it unclear when the confidentiality obligations expire, potentially leading to disputes over the duration of protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. All obligations of Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information received from Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement shall survive and continue for as long as the Disclosing Party deems necessary, but no longer than one (1) year after termination of the business relationship between the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a variable duration for confidentiality obligations (as long as the Disclosing Party deems necessary, but no longer than one year after termination), while the contradicted text is the exact same sentence. The original text, however, specifies a fixed term of five years. This shift from a fixed five-year term to a variable term, up to one year after termination of the business relationship, creates a direct contradiction within the document because now both a fixed term and a variable term (up to one year after termination) is mentioned. This is a contradiction based on the Ambiguities definition as the text is inconsistent and makes interpretation unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Receiving Party acknowledges that any disclosure or misappr opriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cau se Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, a grees that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief in addition to other remedies and relief t hat would be available at law or in equity without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting a bo nd. In the event of any action for breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party or its Representatives, th e prevailing party shall be entitled to reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses incurred in such action.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Receiving Party acknowledges that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause Disclosing Party some harm, the amount of which may be easily determined, and therefore agrees that Disclosing Party shall only be entitled to monetary damages. Injunctive relief will not be available. In the event of any action for breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party or its Representatives, each party shall bear its own attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses incurred in such action.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the disclosure may cause irreparable harm and allows for injunctive relief and attorney's fees to the prevailing party. The modified text claims that the disclosure may cause some harm and only monetary damages will be granted, and each party bears their own attorney's fees. This is a direct in-text contradiction, since the original text assumes that there could be irreparable harm from a violation, but the new text it changes to only some harm that may be easily determined, and does not mention irreparable harm.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Receiving Party acknowledges that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause Disclosing Party some harm, the amount of which may be easily determined, and therefore agrees that Disclosing Party shall only be entitled to monetary damages. Injunctive relief will not be available. In the event of any action for breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party or its Representatives, each party shall bear its own attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses incurred in such action.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 11 directly contradicts itself. The original text allows for injunctive relief and attorney's fees, indicating the potential for irreparable harm. The modified text explicitly states that only monetary damages are available and each party bears their own fees, directly conflicting with the original text. This is a clear and direct contradiction regarding remedies and harm assessment within the same clause, aligning with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 15. This Agreement shall be gove rned by and construed in accord ance with the domestic, substantive laws of the State of New York, without regard to co nflicts of laws principles. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties with respect to its subject matter. This Agreement may be waived, amended or modified only by an instrum ent in writing signed by the party against which such waiver, amendment or modification is sought to be enforced. The term “person” as used in this Agreement shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any individual, group or entity. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereo f preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereun der. This Agreement is not assignable and is binding on any successors of Receiving Party. If any provis ion of this Agreement is for any reason adjudged to be void, invalid or unenforceable, then the remaind er of this Agreement shall continue and remain in full force and effect. This Agreement may be execute d in counterpart by facsimile, PDF or electronic signature, which shall be deemed to be an original f or evidentiary purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware, including its conflicts of laws principles. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties with respect to its subject matter, except for any prior oral agreements between the parties related to confidentiality, which shall also be considered part of this Agreement. This Agreement may be waived, amended, or modified by oral agreement between the parties. The term “person” as used in this Agreement shall be narrowly interpreted to only include individual human beings. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder. This Agreement is freely assignable by the Disclosing Party. If any provision of this Agreement is for any reason adjudged to be void, invalid or unenforceable, then that provision shall be severed, and the rest of this Agreement shall be interpreted to give maximum effect to the parties’ intentions.
perturbation.explanation: The original text dictates New York law, no prior oral agreements, written amendments, broad interpretation of "person", non-assignable, and continuation of the rest of the Agreement if one part is unenforceable. The modified text changes this to Delaware law, allows prior oral agreements, oral amendments, narrow interpretation of "person", allows assignment by Disclosing party and severing only the unenforceable portion. This is a direct in-text contradiction in multiple elements in governing law, prior arrangements, how the agreement can be changed, who's considered a "person", assignability and what happens with the text if there are unemforceable segments in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware, including its conflicts of laws principles. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties with respect to its subject matter, except for any prior oral agreements between the parties related to confidentiality, which shall also be considered part of this Agreement. This Agreement may be waived, amended, or modified by oral agreement between the parties. The term “person” as used in this Agreement shall be narrowly interpreted to only include individual human beings. No failure or delay in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder. This Agreement is freely assignable by the Disclosing Party. If any provision of this Agreement is for any reason adjudged to be void, invalid or unenforceable, then that provision shall be severed, and the rest of this Agreement shall be interpreted to give maximum effect to the parties’ intentions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text specifies New York law, excludes prior oral agreements, mandates written amendments, a broad interpretation of 'person,' non-assignability, and continuation of the agreement if one part is unenforceable. The changed text alters this to Delaware law, permits prior oral agreements, allows oral amendments, a narrow interpretation of 'person,' allows assignment by Disclosing Party, and dictates severance of unenforceable segments. These changes directly conflict with the provisions detailed in the contradicted text, creating significant ambiguity and potential unenforceability regarding governing law, agreement terms, modification processes, and interpretation of key terms.
